name: Vehicle Damage
description: A vehicle durability model where collisions, weapons, terrain hazards, or misuse reduce whole-vehicle or part-specific health and can affect handling, visuals, and repair needs.
design_notes: Vehicle damage can create stakes for reckless driving, combat encounters, and survival travel. The design has to decide whether damage is mostly visual, a repair economy, a handling penalty, or a failure condition. Strong implementations separate impact detection, damage attribution, part state, repair transactions, and replicated presentation so crashes remain understandable and hard to exploit.
design_purpose: Add consequence to collisions, risky routes, and combat pressure.
Connect vehicles to repair costs, crafting, economy, or survival planning.
Give designers a way to differentiate fragile, armored, civilian, and racing vehicles.
player_fantasy: The player feels the machine survive punishment, show scars, and demand care after dangerous driving.

parameters.name: vehicle_id
parameters.description: Vehicle instance receiving collision or hazard damage.

parameters.name: health_component_id
parameters.description: Damage bucket changed by the resolved hit.

parameters.name: collision_impulse_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum resolved contact impulse before damage is considered.

parameters.name: damage_zone_id
parameters.description: Hit region used for part health and visual state.

parameters.name: damage_source_id
parameters.description: Cause label used for attribution, resistances, and analytics.

parameters.name: repair_state_id
parameters.description: Repair workflow state exposed to UI and persistence.

parameters.name: visual_damage_stage_id
parameters.description: Presentation stage matched to health and damage zone.

edge_cases: A vehicle receives several contact impulses in one physics step and should not apply duplicate damage for one crash.
A player exits or swaps seats while the vehicle transitions from damaged to disabled.
A repaired vehicle still has old smoke, tire, or dent presentation attached to pooled objects.
Multiplayer clients disagree about which player caused a collision or explosive hit.
A checkpoint restores vehicle position but not health_component_id and repair_state_id values.
common_bugs: Damage uses raw velocity difference instead of resolved collision impulse, causing harmless scrapes to destroy vehicles.
Part health reaches zero but drivetrain, steering, or wheel constraints continue as if intact.
Repair consumes materials client-side before the server accepts the repair transaction.
Visual damage stages advance on prediction and never roll back after authoritative correction.
Destroyed vehicles keep collision enabled and block level routes after respawn.
balancing_notes: Pure handling penalties can make recovery frustrating; reserve severe penalties for readable impacts or optional simulation modes.
Repair costs should scale with route risk and economy pacing, not punish small cosmetic mistakes excessively.
Damage thresholds need different profiles for racing contact, combat hits, off-road falls, and scripted set pieces.
accessibility_notes: Show damage state through text, icons, sound, and visual stages so it is not dependent on subtle deformation.
Offer reduced handling penalty or assisted recovery options for players who struggle with damaged steering.
Avoid persistent smoke or flashing damage warnings that obscure road visibility.

implementation_notes.unity: Read collision.impulse during OnCollisionEnter or OnCollisionStay, map contacts to damage_zone_id, and process health changes through a VehicleDamageService. Keep repair transactions server-authoritative in Netcode and reset particles, colliders, and wheel constraints when pooled vehicles respawn.
implementation_notes.godot: Use RigidBody3D contact data or area hazard callbacks to create damage events with vehicle_id, damage_source_id, and damage_zone_id. Apply changes in _physics_process order, then emit state change signals for UI, audio, mesh swaps, and save persistence.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Chaos hit events, physical material data, and replicated components for health_component_id state. Server authority should resolve source attribution, part health, and disabled state before multicasting visual_damage_stage_id updates or repair success.
implementation_notes.web: For custom or cannon-like physics, convert contact manifolds into normalized damage events and process them in a deterministic reducer. Store vehicle health, zone states, and repair_state_id in saves and rollback snapshots instead of deriving them from visuals.

relationships.reason: Vehicle damage and world destruction need source attribution and collision impulse ownership.
Vehicle damage needs repair costs or recipes when damaged state can be restored.
Damage feedback helps players distinguish collision damage from combat or environmental wear.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
example_games.note: Includes visible vehicle damage and repair interactions.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
example_games.note: Shows visible vehicle damage from collisions and impacts.

example_games.title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
example_games.note: Shows visible vehicle damage and takedown pressure during pursuits.

example_games.title: BeamNG.drive
example_games.note: Focuses heavily on vehicle deformation and crash behavior.
